name: The Four Elemental Temples
entries: The four elemental cults—Earth, Air, Fire, and Water—operate semi-independently under the Greater Temple's authority. Each is led by a high priest who vies for supremacy, seeking to prove their element's dominance over the others. The four lesser elemental temples—Earth, Air, Fire, and Water—are devoted to the Elder Elemental God, believing their power stems from its boundless elemental chaos. Each faction believes their element is supreme, their rituals, they will eventually awaken (EEG) to reshape the world in their image.

entries.name: The Black Earth (Earth Temple)
entries: Romag (Earth Temple) is a calculating and methodical leader who values stability and secrecy above all else. He strengthens his influence in Nulb through intermediaries and spies, but he fears the Water Temple's growing power.

entries.name: Belief
entries: The Elder Elemental God embodies the foundation of existence. Only through destruction and decay can new empires rise from the ruins of the old.

entries.name: Goal
entries: Weaken Veluna and Furyondy by sabotaging agriculture, inciting earthquakes, and toppling fortifications.

entries.name: Relationships
entries: Feuds with Belsornig and Alrrem while secretly seeking Hedrack's favor.

entries.name: The Howling Hatred (Air Temple)
entries: Kelno (Air Temple) is arrogant and fanatical, convinced that he alone speaks for Elemental Evil. He plots against the Fire and Water Temples through sabotage and subterfuge, though his influence is waning.

entries.name: Belief
entries: The Elder Elemental God manifests as an invisible storm, bringing whispers of chaos and the power to sweep away civilization.

entries.name: Goal
entries: Focuses on controlling the skies, manipulating weather, and summoning devastating winds to erode their enemies' morale.

entries.name: Relationships
entries: Distrusts Belsornig, fears Alrrem, and seeks Romag's alliance.

entries.name: The Eternal Flame (Fire Temple)
entries: Alrrem (Fire Temple) is aggressive and ambitious, forging alliances with fire giants and other destructive forces. He dreams of a world consumed by flame, with the Temple at its heart.

entries.name: Belief
entries: The Elder Elemental God is fire incarnate, consuming all in its path and purging the weak.

entries.name: Goal
entries: Spreading destruction through arson, terror tactics, and fire-wielding warriors. They seek to burn away the old world to make way for the new.

entries.name: Relationships
entries: Holds deep grudges against Romag and Kelno, seeks Belsornig's destruction.

entries.name: The Crushing Wave (Water Temple)
entries: Belsornig (Water Temple) is the most cunning of the four high priests, ruling through an intricate network of spies in Nulb and beyond. His ultimate ambition is to dominate the Temple—even if it means betraying the Elder Elemental God.

entries.name: Belief
entries: The Elder Elemental God resides deep within the endless seas, waiting to drown the world in an eternal deluge.

entries.name: Goal
entries: Flood the lands, sabotage trade routes, and infiltrate coastal cities with spies and saboteurs.

entries.name: Relationships
entries: Seeks to manipulate Kelno and eliminate Alrrem, while keeping Romag at bay.

entries.name: Iuz's Influence
entries: Iuz, the demigod of deceit and domination, sees the Temple of Elemental Evil as a pawn in his grand strategy of conquest. The Temple's clergy believe they serve the Elder Elemental God, but in truth, their spells come from Zuggtmoy, the Demon Queen of Fungi. Iuz aims to subjugate Zuggtmoy while turning the Temple into a launching point for war against Furyondy, Veluna, and Verbobonc.

entries.name: Hedrack's Role
entries: Hedrack, Supreme Commander of the Greater Temple, is Iuz's enforcer, keeping the elemental cults in check while secretly orchestrating Zuggtmoy's release.

entries.name: Political Struggles
entries: Many clerics and warlords within the Temple bristle at Iuz's growing dominance, clinging to their faith in the Elder Elemental God—whether real or imagined. This brewing dissent forces Hedrack to tread carefully, balancing fear and diplomacy to maintain control.

entries.name: Strategic Goal
entries: Iuz's ultimate goal is to forge a portal linking the elemental nodes to his capital, Dorakaa, unleashing his armies upon the heart of the Flanaess.

entries.name: Lolth's Infiltration
entries: As Iuz commands the Temple from above, Lolth, the Demon Queen of Spiders, spins a web of deceit within its ranks. The Temple stands upon an ancient site sacred to the Elder Elemental God, and Lolth is determined to prevent its full resurgence. Holding the key to the Elder Elemental God's prison, she is a crucial force in deciding its fate. Unlike Iuz, who rules through brute strength and fear, Lolth prefers subterfuge, deception, and betrayal.

entries.name: Lareth the Beautiful
entries: A charismatic recruiter and secret agent of Lolth, luring followers into her web of intrigue.

entries.name: Falrinth
entries: A cunning wizard who serves Lolth from the shadows while feigning loyalty to Hedrack.

entries.name: Tactics
entries: Sowing discord through misinformation, eroding Iuz's grip on the Temple, and fueling internal rivalries.
